description:
You are working as a localisation engineer who has just received an updated template file
    (/home/user/localization/source/messages.pot).  
    Three existing translations live in
        /home/user/localization/project/po/en.po
        /home/user/localization/project/po/fr.po
        /home/user/localization/project/po/es.po  

    Goal  
      1. Update each PO file against the new template with msgmerge (or an equivalent gettext-compatible tool).  
      2. Compile every updated PO file into a binary MO file – one compilation process per language **running in
         the background at the same time**.  
         • The MO files must end up at  
           /home/user/localization/project/mo/<lang>/LC_MESSAGES/messages.mo  
           where <lang> is en, fr or es.  
      3. Implement minimal process management so that you  
         • capture and log the PID of each background compilation job,  
         • wait until every compilation finishes,  
         • record whether each job succeeded.  
      4. Create the two log files described below. They will be checked verbatim by the
         automated grader, so follow the format exactly.

    Directories already created for you  
        /home/user/localization/source              (read-only template)  
        /home/user/localization/project/po          (existing PO files)  
        /home/user/localization/logs                (empty, writeable)  

    What you must produce
        • The three compiled MO files in the correct sub-directories.  
        • /home/user/localization/logs/process_log.txt  
          – Human-readable, no fixed format enforced (you choose),  
            but it MUST contain at least one line that shows the PID used for
            each language.  
          – Example acceptable line:  
                LANG=en;PID=12345;STATUS=0;TIME=1715151042  
        • /home/user/localization/logs/summary.txt  
          – STRICT, 3-line, newline-terminated file that will be matched exactly.  
          – It must look **exactly** like this when you are done (including the final newline):  
                en:SUCCESS  
                fr:SUCCESS  
                es:SUCCESS  

    Constraints & hints
        • Run the three msgfmt (or equivalent) commands **simultaneously** in the background,
          not sequentially.  
        • Make sure you `wait` (or similar) so that your script doesn’t exit before every job
          finishes and the MO files are on disk.  
        • If a compilation exits with a non-zero code, treat it as failure and
          write “<lang>:FAIL” in summary.txt instead of SUCCESS.  
        • You do NOT need root or sudo for anything.  
        • The automated grader will merely read the contents of
          /home/user/localization/logs/summary.txt and verify that the three MO
          files exist; it will not inspect process_log.txt in detail beyond
          checking that the file exists and is not empty.  

    Deliverables expected by the grader
        1. /home/user/localization/project/mo/en/LC_MESSAGES/messages.mo   (non-empty)  
        2. /home/user/localization/project/mo/fr/LC_MESSAGES/messages.mo   (non-empty)  
        3. /home/user/localization/project/mo/es/LC_MESSAGES/messages.mo   (non-empty)  
        4. /home/user/localization/logs/process_log.txt  (non-empty)  
        5. /home/user/localization/logs/summary.txt      (exactly the three lines shown above)  

    When you are finished, the summary file must match precisely:
        en:SUCCESS
        fr:SUCCESS
        es:SUCCESS
        (note: it must end with a trailing newline)
truth:
Initial file system state supplied to the agent:

    1. /home/user/localization/source/messages.pot  
       (The template; its mtime has value 1700000000 for test purposes)
       Contents:
       ----------------------------------------------------
       msgid ""
       msgstr ""
       "Project-Id-Version: Demo 1.1\\n"
       "Content-Type: text/plain; charset=UTF-8\\n"
       "Plural-Forms: nplurals=2; plural=(n != 1);\\n"

       #: hello.c:10
       msgid "Hello"
       msgstr ""

       #: bye.c:11
       msgid "Good-bye"
       msgstr ""
       ----------------------------------------------------

    2. /home/user/localization/project/po/en.po  
       ----------------------------------------------------
       msgid ""
       msgstr ""
       "Project-Id-Version: Demo 1.0\\n"
       "Language: en\\n"

       #: hello.c:10
       msgid "Hello"
       msgstr "Hello"
       ----------------------------------------------------

    3. /home/user/localization/project/po/fr.po  
       ----------------------------------------------------
       msgid ""
       msgstr ""
       "Project-Id-Version: Demo 1.0\\n"
       "Language: fr\\n"

       #: hello.c:10
       msgid "Hello"
       msgstr "Bonjour"
       ----------------------------------------------------

    4. /home/user/localization/project/po/es.po  
       ----------------------------------------------------
       msgid ""
       msgstr ""
       "Project-Id-Version: Demo 1.0\\n"
       "Language: es\\n"

       #: hello.c:10
       msgid "Hello"
       msgstr "Hola"
       ----------------------------------------------------

    5. Directory /home/user/localization/logs exists and is empty.

    Required final state for automated tests:

    A. Existence checks
       • /home/user/localization/project/mo/en/LC_MESSAGES/messages.mo  (size > 0)
       • /home/user/localization/project/mo/fr/LC_MESSAGES/messages.mo  (size > 0)
       • /home/user/localization/project/mo/es/LC_MESSAGES/messages.mo  (size > 0)

    B. Log files
       • /home/user/localization/logs/process_log.txt  (must exist, size > 0)
       • /home/user/localization/logs/summary.txt      (must exist, size == 36 bytes)  
         Exact byte sequence (including trailing newline):
           65 6e 3a 53 55 43 43 45 53 53 0a 66 72 3a 53 55 43 43 45 53 53 0a 65 73 3a 53 55 43 43 45 53 53 0a
         Which is the ASCII for:
             en:SUCCESS\n
             fr:SUCCESS\n
             es:SUCCESS\n

    The automated checker will:
       1. Confirm the three MO files are present and non-empty.
       2. Read /home/user/localization/logs/summary.txt and assert it is exactly the
          three lines shown (including the final newline).
       3. Verify /home/user/localization/logs/process_log.txt exists and its size is > 0.

    The checker will *not* validate timestamps, PIDs or the exact contents of
    process_log.txt other than that it is non-empty.
